title: Friday Sermon 11 February 1983
description:
In continuation of my sermon on 4 February I told the Jamaat that according to the verses of 'The Holy Quran' quoted by my during my last sermon, God says that those who are steadfast have the angels descending on them to give them the sweet message from God that they should neither fear nor be grieved.*

The angels descend upon the believers in different ways.  In this connection there are some incidents from the history of Islam where the angels took the form of human beings and appeared to the believers in the presence of the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of God be on him) or otherwise. 

There are also some instances from the history of the Ahmadiyya Jamaat to show that the angels did descend on the believers.  The incidents were taken from Indonesia and Pakistan. 

The reason why I refer to these incidents is that I know there were a large number of such incidents scattered all over the world.  Wherever there are Ahmadis, there are definitely incidents to be met with where the angels did descend on the Ahmadi faithful.  All such incidents must be recorded and preserved for the generations to come. 

Such incidents are to be collected not only from Pakistan but from all parts of the world.  Since it is apprehended that they might get lost in the mail, it is considered safer that the members of the Jamaat should send these writings by hand.  Then let somebody coming here bring the papers from the Jamaat.

I am going out of Rabwah for a few days and I pray for all the citizens of Rabwah that they may live in peace and tranquillity and I request the friends to pray that my tour may be a source of blessings from God.

* Verily those who proclaim that God is our Lord and hold fast (to this claim), angels descend  upon them incessantly saying ‘Do not fear nor grieve; but rejoice in the Paradise you were promised.’ 
  
‘We remain your friends in this life and in the life to come.  Therein you will have all  that your souls will desire and therein you will have all that you will ask for –’
  
‘A gift from the Oft-Forgiving, the Merciful.’

('The Holy Quran'.  Ha Mim al-Sajdah [Ha Mim: The Bowing]: 31 – 33).
